Step 3: The Big Problem (Part 2)
ALL CONTENT © COPYRIGHT 2011 AND BEYOND | JON BENSON 3X
FORMULAS, INC. | ALL RIGHTS RESERVED | NO DUPLICATION ALLOWED
Jon: 	This is step 3, which is blame this.
Now, what do I mean by blame this? Well, in the first video, you saw
how we build a lie. We discover the lie, then we build the lie. And
we’re talking about creating a conspiracy. And remember that magical
word, “conspiracy.” Well, let’s get into that right now.
It is human nature to assign blame. It is human nature. And your
prospects tend to blame themselves. I think most people tend to
blame themselves on some level.
Now, the people that do that, from a psychological point of view, are
probably not going to be as vocal about it, but the last thing we want
your reader to be doing, your viewer, your prospect, your potential
customer, whatever, is to blame themselves.
What you want to do, and this is a little hint that very few marketers
know about, or at least very few marketers will tell you, is the only time
you want your prospect to feel any sort of blame, is when they don’t
take your advice for taking action in your grand offer.
Then, you’re going to blame the hell out of them. That’s what you’re
going to do.
But we want to remove the blame, for now, because that opens the
mind up to new decisions.
If you are blaming yourself for something, it is human nature to feel
less empowered and to shut down your own ability to make a decision.
That’s why we remove the blame, and we put it on something else.

-- 1 of 8 --

2
Now, we don’t do that unethically. We come back and we say, “Look,
now that you know the truth,” and we haven’t even got to the truth part
of this, we will later in this video, “once you know the truth, you have to
take action. It’s now your responsibility to take action.”
But, we need to let them know that it’s not all their fault. In many
cases, it’s not their fault at all.
So, there are 3 parts to blame this. “If you want to blame something,
blame blank,” and we’ll get into specifically what that means. Why that
is to blame, whatever you’re saying is to blame, and proof that this is to
blame, if proof is possible.
We’re going to go through each of these 3 parts right now. It’s in the
mind map. In case you don’t have it in front of you, you might want to
grab it.
So, part one, if you want to blame, in other words if you really have to
blame something, don’t blame yourself. Provide something other than
themselves to blame for their pain and lack of results. That’s what you
want to do.
Here’s some example slides on how this would play out.
“If you really want to blame something for your struggles with weight
lose, don’t blame yourself, blame the tabloids and the media, like
television commercials and ‘fitness magazines,’ and cheesy Hollywood
diets. That’s who is to blame.”
You see what I’ve done here? I’m going to go back and make sure
that this is very clear. I am creating a conspiracy. “Don’t blame
yourself, there’s something much bigger than you out there that’s
causing the issues. Blame the tabloids, blame the media, blame the
Hollywood diets, blame the fad diets, you can blame all of that, that’s
what this isn’t your fault. You’ve been mislead, you’ve been lied to.”
This is building a conspiracy.
Now, why is blank to blame? This is where we twist the conspiracy
screw very, very tight. And this is kind of fun. This is where we’re
going to explain why your villain, your create a villain in a conspiracy,
and why this villain is the one to blame.
So, here’s some example slides, “You’ve been bombarded for almost
as long as you’ve been alive with false, misleading claims from these
crooks.”
Now, I want you to analysis the wordsmithing that I’m doing here.
Bombarded, talk about a power word! I could come up with a list of 10-

-- 2 of 8 --

3
million power words. We have a list for you in the download section.
But bombarded is an awesome one. “You’ve been bombarded for
almost as long as you’ve been alive…” – that sounds awful, doesn’t it?
– “with false, misleading claims,” that’s redundancy on purpose, by the
way, false, misleading, I know it’s redundant, but that’s fine, “from
these crooks.”
Now, notice I am putting the media and fitness magazines, in my
example, and Hollywood fad diets. I’m labeling these perpetrators as
crooks. They are the villain. That’s important, these crooks. This is
why they’re crooks, “they depend on your suffering to make a buck.”
This is what you want to do. Now, you can see the conspiracy pattern.
“Don’t blame yourself. If you have to blame something, blame the
media, blame the cheesy diet magazines. They depend on your
suffering to make a buck.”
In other words, “They will put you into a loop.” In my case, I will say,
“They’ll put you into a loop of starvation diets, then they’ll follow it up
with, ‘Oh, the diet didn’t work because you need to take this pill,’”
I will go into an elaborate, truthful, by the way, explanation of why
these industries are to blame for my particular product. You need to
do this for your own.
But, this is setting up the entire thing. Now, this is why this is we’re
going on further into why this is to blame. “All this information out
there, that the media loves to exploit,” notice the words I’m using –
exploit, that’s like, eh, “is enough to confuse anyone, and it confused
me for years.”
In the last video, I mentioned the fact that you want to have an
inclusion statement, the fact that you too got duped by this, because
you are building rapport by saying, “Me, too.” “This happened to me,
too. In fact, this would confuse anyone.” What am I doing?
All throughout these slides, I am removing any sort of blame, any sort
of guilt, any sort of, “Wow, I must be an idiot for believing this.” I’m
removing all of that. I’m doing it proactively. You need to be doing it
proactively in your VSL, because it is building rapport.
In essence, you are talking to someone as if they were your child or
your best friend.
For those of you have children, if your child comes home and you
know that they feel horrible about something that happened in school,
the tendency for most parents would be to sit down and go, “Young

-- 3 of 8 --

4
Billy, this isn’t your fault, you’re not to blame for this.” Now, let’s
assume it really isn’t their fault, “This is the fault of this bully over here.
This isn’t your fault, this bully is the bad guy.”
That’s the same kind of mentality that you want to take when you’re
talking to a prospect.
“This isn’t your fault for believing that you had to starve yourself,
because the media’s been lying to you for as long you’ve been alive,
and they are crooks. They depend on your suffering. And, you know
what? It confused me, too.”
By the way, if it confused you, if you believed this lie, it makes it even
more powerful. If you didn’t believe it, don’t say it. But what you can
say, if you did not believe the lie that you’re talking about, you can say,
I know so many people who believed this lie.”
“So many smart, savvy people fell into this.” This is another excellent
way of wording this, by the way, is to say, “Look, a lot of smart people
bought into this lie, so don’t feel bad. It confuses a lot of smart
people.”
So, again here, you want to take them off the hook, and this is
essential if you want your prospect to trust their own intuition, they’re
own ability to make a smart decision.
Now, part 3 of this 3-part process is to prove that blank is to blame, if
you have the proof. If you don’t, you can skip this section. If there’s no
proof of it, you can’t.
I’m going to give you an example of proof however, and it’s pretty
compelling.
Here’s the example: “A study done in 2007, printed in Psychology
Today revealed that what we see in the media, even if we’re not paying
total attention to it…” Notice the really sinister red words, “paying total
attention?” And, by the way, I hope you are.
But, paying total attention. This is what I chose to highlight here. Kind
of clever, now you’re seeing the 3X VSL subliminal technique at its
finest. “This study showed that even if we’re just partially paying
attention, it influences our decision-making and beliefs to a remarkable
degree; so much, that test subjects reported this,” anchor drop,
“Reading 2 or 3 articles in a tabloid magazine over the course of 17
months was enough to change their minds on how to lose weight.”
That’s a pretty staggering 3 slides of proof, don’t you agree? It is. One
of my products is talking about weight loss.

-- 4 of 8 --

5
Now, I want to be honest with you here, I completely made this up, I
completely made this up, although there are tons of articles that would
back this up. When I’m creating the course, I just wanted to show you
how I would create something that is “proof.”
You’ll never read this in any of my video sales letters, on any diet book,
because this isn’t true. What is true is, if you’re interested, people
have studied how much the media affects our ability to make
decisions. But this would take 4 or 5 hours of research to find.
But if you can find proof like that, or any sort of proof, a study, an
article, a 6-out-of-10 surveyed, whatever you can do, you can prove it.
If not, you can skip this step.
Step 4 is the big truth. We’ve established the lie, right? We’ve gone
through how you describe your lie, we’ve told them that it isn’t their
fault, we’ve let them a little off the hook, and now we’re telling them,
“Guess who’s to blame? There’s something else to blame that’s bigger
than you.”
“So, what’s the truth? If it’s not me, and if it’s something else, what is
the actual truth that falsifies this big lie that you’re talking about?” Well,
the truth is a big picture overview of one of your 3 tips. That’s all it is.
The truth is just simply telling the truth, and most of the time you can
get this big picture overview right from the 3 tip exercises that we’ve
talked about already.
Now, here’s the example slides using one of my 3 tips. “The truth,”
highlighted red, naturally, “is that you should never, ever starve
yourself, even for a few days. Why?” anchor drop, “When your body
senses extreme hunger, it shuts down its fat-burning hormones. Plus,”
anchor drop, “you’re body begins to store more fat, because it thinks
you’re going through a famine. And this is a throw back to our earlier
days, when food was scarce, and our love handles and our thunder
thighs were required for survival.”
Now, I’m going to go back here. This is essential stuff, all the anchor
drops that I’m doing here. Because when you start going into the truth
elements of things, you want people to continue to watch.
Now, most people are going to be very in tune to what you’re saying,
because if you’ve lead them down the lie path correctly, the truth is it’s
going to make them feel good, and you’re going to give them some
answers.
But, we still use a lot of anchor drops in this section, because we want
to continue pushing forward to the conclusion of this truth. In other

-- 5 of 8 --

6
words, “Now that I know what the truth is, what do I do about it?” Well,
that, is the bigger solution. That’s coming up next, in the next video.
This is all factual, by the way. None of this is made up. Your body
shuts down fat-burning hormones, and it begins to store more fat,
because back in the olden days, back in the caveman days, back when
we lived on a savanna, in the Pleistocene era, or whatever, we needed
our fat for survival.
This is why starving yourself, whenever people thought they were
starving, your body would start hoarding onto it’s fat source. Well, I
just informed them about that.
Here’s the thing that you’ve got to avoid. You’ve got to avoid thinking
that everyone knows this.
One of the big, big cardinal sins in writing a video sales letter is to
assume that, “Everyone already knows this already, why should I even
bother telling them?” The truth of the matter is, sad as it is to say, that
most people do not know this at all; and even if they do know this, they
need to be reminded of it.
There’s a saying that I like to use a lot, it’s an old saying, and it says,
“It’s more important to be reminded than informed.” So, reminding
your users is very, very powerful.
So, that is how I go into the big truth, and then there’s step 5, one last
step, and that is open the loop. We’re opening the loop, again. We
open a lot of loops, but this one is going into the bigger solution. And
this is very simple.
If you have 1,2,3 lies, it doesn’t matter, if you have one, you would do it
on your first lie, if you have 3 you would do it on your 3rd lie. Well,
whatever it is, on your last lie, you want to open a loop into step 4 of
the 5-step process, which is the bigger solution.
And all you do to do this, it’s so easy, all you have to do is just one
sentence. And this sets up your prospect to expect answers to the big
problem. That’s the key, you’ve got to set them up to go, “Okay, I
know what the truth is now, but what’s the answer? If what I’ve
believed is a lie, and you just told me why it’s a lie, what do I do about
it?”
Again, just telling someone that, “Oh, you might have believed this, but
this is the truth,” and they go, “Okay, great. Well, then what do I do?”
that’s the natural response, “What do I do next?” That’s the bigger
solution, which we’re going to cover in the next video.

-- 6 of 8 --

7
And this sets your prospect up to expect the answers in your 3 tips.
You’ve already done the 3 tip exercise, so this is where we set them up
to expect this.
Now, here’s some example slides. You can do this in one slide, but I
did it in a few.
“Now,” notice what I’ve done here. Remember, we start sentences
with, yes, now, listen, etc, quite a few times in a VSL. I highlighted it in
red.
“Now” brings them into the present. “Now that you know the problem,
that your weight loss has been held back due to the lies you’ve been
told, let’s dive into the specific types I have for you today.”
“These are the same tips that countless thousands have used to
successfully achieve the weight loss and body they desire and
deserve,” using those words again, “And the most important of these
tips is my caloric staggering principle.”
You need to look back, if you haven’t got the mind map in front of you,
get the mind maps out, and look back when we talk about building the
USP.
What in my USP was my primary benefit? You need to write down
your primary benefit, because I want to open a loop using the primary
benefit. “The most important of these 3 tips I’m going to share with you
is my caloric staggering principle.”
All that is, is the driving force behind my primary benefit. So, in other
words, the big how-to tip. You’ve already done this, by the way, the 3
tips. You’ve already done it. We’re setting the reader up to simply be
introduced into this.
So, I’m going to go back through these slides one more time, because
this could be done in one slide. All you have to say is like, “I’m about
to share 3 tips with you, and the most important of which is my caloric
staggering tip.”
You could just say it that easy, if you wanted to. I just drug it out over
a few slides, to show you the flexibility that you have. “Now that you
know the problem, that your weight loss has been held back due to lies
you’ve been told,” that’s the problem, “let’s dive into some specific tips,
the solution that I have for you today.” In fact, you could even say that.
“Let’s dive into the solution. I have 3 specific tips for you today.” That
works just as well, if not better.
“These are the same tips,” now this is where you get into some pretty

-- 7 of 8 --

8
cool wordsmithing. You can actually set up your tips by using a slide
like this. This is not make-or-break on a VSL, but it certainly is good.
“These are the same tips that countless thousands have used…”
Again, inclusion. Okay? Inclusion. “…to successfully achieve the
weight loss and body they desire and deserve. And the most important
of these tips is my caloric staggering principle.”
Why do I put that there? You know where it comes from. It comes
from the driving force behind your primary benefit. If that sounds
vague to you, then open your USP exercise and look back over it. You
have a primary benefit that has a driving force.
What makes your primary benefit work is the driving force. In my case,
my primary benefit was “you get to eat your favorite foods and lose
weight. And the reason that works,” I coined a phrase, remember
when we coined phrases?
If you can coin a phrase, great. If you can’t, it’s no big deal. But
coining a phrase is powerful, and my coined phrase was the caloric
staggering principle.
So, I’ve already opened the loop in the 3 tip exercise itself.
So, the very first phrase in my 3-tip exercise is, “I’m going to cover
more about this in just a few minutes. But first, let me tell you what to
avoid.”
See, you’ve already done the homework for this section. You’ve
already done it. It’s already built. All you need to do is set it up.
And this is setting up the open-the-loop section to the bigger solution.
Now, here’s the conclusion here. This leads you into the opening
transition for the 3 tips, which you’ve already written, like I said. And
your 3 tips are going to make up 95% of the entire bigger solution step,
which comes up in the next video.

-- 8 of 8 --
